means: Alzheimer's Disease clinical outcome or disease progression status post-mortem. Used to classify participants into "control", "AD", "Other"

related_concept.name: Braak stage for presence of tau protein accompanying neurofibrillary degeneration in brain
CERAD scoring for presence of neuritic plaques

derivation_rule: Derived harmonized outcomes based on CERAD neuritic plaque score and Braak stage, or NINCDS-ADRD criteria for Mayo. The following criteria seek to harmonize outcomes between cohorts as closely as possible, with Mayo’s neuropath-based diagnoses (as determined by Mayo specialists) set as the standard to match. These thresholds will also exclude individuals with age-related tauopathies from being classified as AD cases or controls. sh, MSSM, Columbia derived outcome criteria: AD: Braak ≥ 4, CERAD = Frequent or Moderate Control: Braak ≤ 3, CERAD = Sparse or None Other: all other UFL (from Mayo): some patients from UFL were not consistent with other MayoDx criteria (i.e. 8 individuals had Braak < 4 and a mayoDx==AD). These individuals were assigned ‘Other’ criteria in the ADoutcome variable to harmonize more closely with MayoDx criteria.
